texts.full_text: Book Blog "Fiction reveals truths that reality obscures." ~Jessamyn West About Me Book Reviews Review Policy Give Aways Contact Author Interviews Home About Me Author Interviews Classic Lit. Review Policy Contact Misc. Links List of Authors Thursday, May 10, 2012 Review + Giveaway: Promises by Amber Garr Promises Book One of The Syrenka Series By Amber Garr Genre: Fantasy/paranormal YA Sometimes following your heart can end up destroying the ones you love. When seventeen-year-old Eviana Dumahl is faced with the responsibility of an arranged marriage and clan leadership, she is forced to choose between the life required of a mermaid and one of a teenage girl simply infatuated with the wrong guy. Kain, her devoted fiancé would make a wonderful husband except that she’s been in love with Brendan, a shape-shifting selkie, ever since they were kids. Choosing to abandon her family, her clan, and her life with Kain will have dire consequences far beyond anything she could have imagined. A war is brewing amongst the clans and Eviana unwittingly becomes a pawn in the intricate schemes of a twisted mastermind. With Brendan’s life on the line, she has no choice but to involve those who she once considered friends. Amidst encountering senselescreatures that threaten their lives at every turn while escaping dangerous selkie hunters preying upon them like dogs after cats and dealing with mermen out for revenge they must find a way not only save Brendan’s life-but also Eviana' soul as well; one which has become entwined in the very war itself. Promises is available from: Amazon | Barnes & Noble Review My Ra

texts.full_text: for it. You've Got Mail. Not! 10 Aug, 2017 The tech giant started an email service that offered users an @facebook.com address, in November 2010. However, it was quietly dropped after Facebook admitted that a majority of people weren't using it. Riff Goes O'er The Cliff! 10 Aug, 2017 Just a few months after launching Snapchat competitor, Riff, a collaborative video creation app for both iOS and Android users in 2015, Facebook plugged the plug on it. However, the tech giant said that most of the features of the app had been integrated into the main Facebook app and Messenger. No Takers For Paper 10 Aug, 2017 Facebook shut down Paper, a stand-alone iOS app that was part News Feed, part newsreader, in 2016, two years after its launch. The app changed the user experience with customisable sections for politics, technology, food, and other subjects. However, the app that impressed critics failed to attract a large audience and was consequently shut down. east overhead possible: #include <iostream> using namespace std; template<typename U> class A { public: A(const U& u) : u(u) {}; template <typename T> T get_as() { T* t; // <- ugly return get(t); } U get(U*) { return u; } int get(int*) { return i; } double get(double*) { return d; } private: U u; int i = 5; double d = 3.14; }; int main() { A<string> a("name"); cout << a.get_as<double>() << '
'; cout << a.get_as<int>() << '
'; cout << a.get_as<string>() << '
'; return 0; } I guess it's purely aesthetic with a pointer, because it's probably optimized, but still I wonder. I also guess this might be impossible, because (AFAIK) this argument goes through stack. So technically there is always this "overhead". Is this right? Not sure if this should be another question, but is there a way to expRess, "just pick up this overload" with out trickery? PS. I figured out I could use T*(0) in the "ugly" line, but it's probably even less readable. A: You can't get any clearer or more efficient than passing an empty tag struct: template<class T> struct tag {}; double get(tag<double>) { return d; } template <typename T> T get_as() { return get(tag<T>{}); } A

texts.full_text: In 1822, Caleb King's son-in-law Daniel Farrar donated a plot of land where a brick church was built. Kingston was prosperous from 1800 to 1824, when it had three stores and a number of shops, with a population of about 150. A tornado in 1840 destroyed most of Kingston. By the early 1900s, all that remained of Kingston was a doctor's office, the church, and one dwelling. Present day Kingston Methodist Church and Cemetery are still located south of the settlement, and were placed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1982. A station belonging to the Kingston Volunteer Fire Department is located west of the settlement. References Category:Unincorporated communities

texts.full_text: s she gently took care of her babies. Feeding them, pushing them in the stroller, putting them to sleep. I watched her as she made shapes out of play doh and colored in strawberry shortcake. I watched as she tucked her long hair behind her ears, while it continued to fall in her face as she bent down to her babies. I watched her as she giggled and played, her face lighting up with joy. I watched her, and I marveled at how much she had grown, how much she had changed. But in those moments, I also saw the challenges she faced, the weight of her responsibilities. I saw the worry in her eyes as she tended to her babies, the determination in her face as she navigated the ups and downs of motherhood. I saw the love she had for her children, the love that had only grown and deepened over the years. And as I watched her, I felt a profound sense of gratitude. Gratitude for the gift of motherhood, for the love that had brought us to that moment.

texts.full_text: hannel 5 went on the air in 1947 on the back lot of Paramount Pictures in Hollywood. At the time, it was the first commercial television station west of the Mississippi. The station has played a major role in the history of Los Angeles television news ever since. Two years later, the station rose to national prominence with its live coverage of the unsuccessful rescue effort for Kathy Fiscus, a 3-year-old girl who fell more than 90 feet down an abandoned well while playing with her older sister and two cousins in a San Marino field overgrown with weeds. What followed was a drama that played out for 27 1/2 hours on live television, back when there were only about 20,000 TV sets in Los Angeles County. The real-life story, with its sad ending, ultimately showed that television had a power beyond what most broadcasters had imagined. In the decades after that event, KTLA's News at 10 with anchor Hal Fishman dominated the evening TV news ratings. KTLA scored another national milestone when it was the first station in the country to use an electron microscope on live television to show viewers the size of a smallpox virus. Throughout its history, KTLA has provided coverage for numerous high-profile events, including the 1984 Summer Olympics and more recently, the LA riots and the O.J. Simpson murder trial. It's a station with a rich history that continues to be a vital part of the Los Angeles media landscape today. texts.full_text: sed to detect cerebral vasospasm and concussion. We explored this technique in a cohort of code stroke patients to see whether a signature could be identified to aid in the diagnosis of large vessel occlusion (LVO) stroke. A military-grade three-axis accelerometer was affixed to a headset. Accelerometer and electrocardiogram (ECG) outputs were digitized at 1.6 kHz. We call the resulting digitized signals the "headpulse." Three-minute recordings were performed immediately after computed tomography (CT) angiography (CTA) and/or immediately before and after attempted mechanical thrombectomy in patents with suspected stroke. The resulting waveforms were inspected by eye and then subjected to supervised machine learning (MATLAB Classification 2.0) to develop and validate a model to detect LVO. Ten (7 men) patients were studied. The predominant LVO stroke was in the left middle cerebral artery. The model was 0.95 accurate in the 0.2 to 2.0 Hz (2 Hz) frequency range. texts.full_text: followed four groups of golfers as they navigated the course. Our task was simple: time each player's routine before and after their shots to gauge overall pace. This involved noting when a golfer reached his ball, decided on club choice, took practice swings (if any), aimed, executed the shot, then repeated for all 45 players across nine holes. It was an arduous but illuminating experience that revealed some interesting insights into player routines and habits during play. I ended up with sore feet, a ruined pair of Nikes and a watermelon-colored sunburn on my calves despite liberal sunscreen application, but I also had some pretty extensive data on how long Tour players took over their shots. texts.full_text: pinMode and digitalWrite are unsafe to use in a constructor, since there is a chance they may be negated during the startup. That is why the majority of Arduino classes have a .begin() method which is called from setup(). The proper method for using classes with pin control and operations is: The constructor takes the pins to use The constructor saves those pins in variables The begin() method is called in setup() The pins are configured appropriately. For example: class myClass { private: int _myPin; public: myClass(int pin) : {} void begin() { pinMode(_myPin, OUTPUT); digitalWrite(_myPin, HIGH); } }; myClass p(4); void setup() { p.begin(); } void loop() { } The object instance p is in the global scope and can be referenced from any function. The pin is configured only when setup() is run. The startup sequence, more specifically, is typically this: The memory used by global and static variables is "zeroed". Any pre-defined variable values (BSS) are copied from flash into RAM. Any object constructors are called. The main() function is called (main startup function). The init() function is called which configures the Arduino platform. The initVariant() function is called which performs any board specific setup routines. texts.full_text: e. But there is another option: merge or acquire. And so those thousands of players started dwindling down to hundreds. But it wasn’t enough. Even though crude prices began to recover from 2016, it never again reached the dizzying levels of the boom years. Debt accumulated turned into debt to be repaid. And the financial community got wiser. Instead of being blinded by the promise of shale volumes, investors and shareholders started demanding value and dividends. Easy capital was no longer available to a small shale driller. And because of that no new small shale drillers emerged. Instead, the big boys arrived. Because shale oil and gas still held vast potential, the likes of ExxonMobil, Shell and Chevron started moving in. ExxonMobil went as far as calling the Permian its ‘future’ (though this was in the days before its super discoveries in Guyana were announced). With consolidation came cohesion. Instead of a complicated patchwork of small plots, a US shale operator’s modus operandi was now to look to its left or right for land that someone else owned which could be stitched up into its own acreage forming a contiguous asset. And so those hundreds of players started becoming dozens. In late 2020, this drive ratcheted up as the prolonged Covid-caused fuels depression freed up plenty of candidates for deep-pocketed players. ConocoPhillips bought Concho Resources for US$9.7 billion. Pioneer Natural Resources snapped up Parsley Energy for US$4.5 billion. RefTurbonilla manolorolani is a species of sea snail, a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Pyramidellidae, the pyrams and their allies. Description The shell of grows to a length of approximately 3.4 mm. It is characterized by a small, conical shape with a pointed apex and a smooth, glossy surface. The shell typically exhibits a pale to translucent coloration, with faint growth lines visible under magnification. The sculpture may include fine axial ribs or spiral threads, though these features can vary among individuals. Distribution This species is known to inhabit the Pacific Ocean, specifically in waters off the Solomon Islands and Fiji. It is likely associated with shallow to moderate-depth marine environments, though precise ecological data remains limited. Ecology and Behavior As a member of the Pyramidellidae family, is presumed to be a predatory or parasitic species, feeding on other small mollusks or benthic invertebrates. Its small size suggests it plays a role in benthic food webs, though specific interactions remain understudied. We explore several pattern recognition techniques, including neural networks, decision trees, clustering algorithms and nearest neighbor searches, with the intention of designing a system capable of efficiently extracting meaningful information from high dimensional data sets. Our focus is on developing novel strategies that are efficient to train and implement, as well as robust against noise in the input data. To test our algorithm, we use a publically available dataset known as CIFAR-10, which consists of 60 thousand labeled images representing ten different classes of objects. The performance of our system is evaluated using various metrics such as precision, recall, F-measure and accuracy. Our results indicate that the proposed model achieves high levels of classification accuracy on noisy data with little or no preprocessing required.' keywords: pattern recognition; neural networks; decision trees; clustering algorithms; nearest neighbor searches; CIFAR-10 dataset. --- abstract: 'In this work, we develop a framework for predicting stock market prices using machine learning techniques. The proposed model is trained to learn the patterns of historical stock prices and their corresponding future prices based on various financial indicators including moving averages, volatility indices and trading volumes. We utilize several machine learning algorithms such as linear regression, logistic regression, support vector machines and neural networks to build our prediction models. Our experiments reveal that a neural network model with a two-layer perceptron architecture achieves the best performance in terms of mean absolute error (MAE) on the test dataset. The model is further optimized by tuning its hyperparameters such as learning rate and number of hidden units through cross-validation techniques.' keywords: stock market prediction; machine learning; linear regression; logistic regression; support vector machines; neural networks; moving

Lieut. C.H. Carlton, Commanding. September 17, 1862.. Between 3 and 4 p.m., of the 17th, the 4th U.S. Infantry took position on the right (north) of the Boonsboro Pike, near the Bloody Lane. Companies G. I. and K. under command of Lieut. Carlton, were advanced as skirmishers, their left resting on the pike at this point. The right extending northwardly across open ground in the direction of Piper's Lane. In this position they engaged the Confederate skirmishers at a rail fence, 75 yards distant from the center of the line. The center was enfiladed by canister from the enemy's guns on Cemetery Hill, south of this road, and suffered severely. texts.full_text: closely aligned with prevailing private market fees – known as “parity” reimbursement policies - evidence suggests that dental practices are more willing to participate, thus increasing patient access. In addition to public health centers and educational institutions’ contributions towards filling these gaps through targeted programming directed at medically underserved areas (MUAs), other stakeholders including non-profit organizations have created free or low-cost care programs such as community-sponsored initiatives targeting specific populations like seniors on fixed incomes. Emergency rooms within hospitals also serve a safety-net function in dental emergencies, although this role is not intended to replace comprehensive oral healthcare and may lead individuals through an expensive path that often fails at preventative approaches. In light of these considerations for policy recommendations aimed at enhancing the workforce dedicated towards providing essential care under such conditions are multifold. Firstly establishing parity reimbursement policies state-wide can incentivize more providers into Medicaid programs, improving both access and quality by bringing rates closer to those offered in private practice settings thus making participation financially viable rather than punitive. Secondly increasing funding for dental school outreach initiatives like student- or residency-based clinics as well enhancing their operating budgets ensures that educational institutions have the capacity not just expand these services but sustain them long term. Third, streamlining enrollment processes into safety-net care facilities along with improving patients’ awareness of available resources would help in mitigating barriers to access while fostering trust within communities. Is that correct? When you make an Impression Check, what direction are you setting the strength of (the relationship to you or the relationship from you)? A: Impressions Checks work like this: if someone is making an Impression check, they are trying to make an impression on another character. If that other character doesn't want to be impressed by them (or isn't paying attention), then no relationship forms and nothing happens. If the person being checked does want to pay attention, though, then you roll a d6 for each of their Feelings towards you: if they're positive, it's +1; neutral

texts.full_text: improved and substantially self contained test unit or kit for collecting and analyzing a biological specimen. Medical swabs are generally known in the art for use in collecting biological specimens from a patient for further analysis. Such medical swabs commonly comprises a fibrous swab tip at one end of an elongated stick or shaft which is manually handled to contact the swab tip with selected tissue cells or other biological specimen obtained, for example, within the ear, nose or throat of a patient. As a result, some of the targeted biological specimen adheres to the swab tip which then can be contacted with one or more chemical reagents to indicate the presence of infection or other information regarding about that particular sample. The traditional process for collecting and analyzing such specimens often involves multiple steps. Firstly, a medical professional must collect the specimen using an appropriate tool like a swab. This requires skillful handling in order not just to gather but also preserve as much of the biological material from its original environment without contamination or degradation before it can be analyzed. texts.full_text: , as the protein is transiently expressed in immature neurons during their migration and maturation phases [@pone.0059269-Altman1]. Dcx serves as a reliable molecular marker for neurogenesis, with its expression peaking in newly generated granule cells of the DG and gradually declining as neurons mature. This dynamic expression pattern allows researchers to quantify neurogenic activity in both physiological and pathological contexts. For instance, studies in rodents have shown that Dcx levels are elevated in response to environmental enrichment or physical exercise, conditions known to enhance NG, while they are reduced in models of chronic stress or neurodegenerative diseases [@pone.0059269- vanPraag1]. In the context of neuropsychiatric disorders, altered Dcx expression has been observed in postmortem human hippocampal tissue from individuals with depression or schizophrenia, suggesting a potential link between impaired NG and disease pathology [@pone.0059269-Grayson1]. Furthermore, preclinical studies indicate that pharmacological or genetic modulation of Dcx can influence NG rates, offering a potential therapeutic avenue for restoring neurogenic deficits. However, the precise mechanisms by which Dcx regulates neuronal survival, integration, and function remain incompletely understood, necessitating further investigation into its role in both healthy and diseased brains.
